items.situation_en: Joining class, asking questions, and confirming assignments.
items.situation_zh: 参加课堂、提问并确认作业要求。

useful_expressions.usage_zh: 课堂上向老师提问时使用，语气礼貌。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Used politely when asking a teacher a question in class.

useful_expressions.usage_zh: 没听懂课程内容时礼貌请求重复。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Used when you didn't understand and need a polite re-explanation.

useful_expressions.zh: 作业提交日期是什么时候？
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 询问作业截止日期时使用。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Used to ask about assignment due dates.

example_dialogue.speaker: 学生
example_dialogue.zh: 作业提交日期是下周一吗？
example_dialogue.en: Is the assignment deadline next Monday?

vocabulary.note_zh: 常用于课堂、会议、面试等正式场景。
vocabulary.note_en: Commonly used in class, meetings, and interviews.

vocabulary.note_zh: 泛指学生需要完成的课业任务。
vocabulary.note_en: Refers to academic tasks or homework assigned to students.

vocabulary.note_zh: 常用于提交作业、报告或申请材料。
vocabulary.note_en: Used for assignment, report, or application submission dates.

items.ai_explanation_en: Use polite endings like -습니다 or -실 수 있을까요 when speaking in class or to instructors.
items.ai_explanation_zh: 课堂上或向讲师提问时，应使用 -습니다 或 -실 수 있을까요 等礼貌语尾。

items.situation_en: Emailing or speaking to a professor about office hours and feedback.
items.situation_zh: 与教授预约面谈、说明问题并请求建议。

useful_expressions.en: I would like to schedule a meeting.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 用于向教授预约个别面谈。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Polite request to schedule an individual meeting with a professor.

useful_expressions.usage_zh: 请求教授对作业或研究给予意见。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Ask a professor for feedback on your work or research.

useful_expressions.usage_zh: 提醒对方查看邮件时使用，语气非常礼貌。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Politely remind someone to check their email.

example_dialogue.speaker: 学生
example_dialogue.zh: 教授，我想预约面谈时间。
example_dialogue.en: Professor, I would like to schedule a meeting.

example_dialogue.speaker: 学生
example_dialogue.en: Thank you. I will visit you then.

vocabulary.note_zh: 通常指与教授或上司的一对一会谈。
vocabulary.note_en: One-on-one meeting, usually with a professor or supervisor.

vocabulary.note_zh: 常用于学术和工作场景。
vocabulary.note_en: Commonly used in academic and professional settings.

vocabulary.note_zh: 可用于预约面谈、餐厅或服务。
vocabulary.note_en: Used for booking meetings, restaurants, or services.

items.ai_explanation_en: When contacting professors, keep the message concise, formal, and clear about time and purpose.
items.ai_explanation_zh: 联系教授时应简洁、正式，并明确时间和目的。

items.situation_en: Finding books, reserving study rooms, and asking about borrowing rules.
items.situation_zh: 在图书馆咨询座位、借书、打印或学习空间。

useful_expressions.usage_en: Used when borrowing a book from the library.

useful_expressions.en: Where can I reserve a study room?
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 询问自习室预约地点或方式。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Ask where or how to book a study room.

useful_expressions.en: I would like to check the due date.
useful_expressions.usage_en: Check the return due date for borrowed materials.

example_dialogue.speaker: 职员
example_dialogue.en: Yes, please show your student ID.

vocabulary.note_zh: 韩国大学图书馆通常需要学生证进入。
vocabulary.note_en: University libraries in Korea usually require a student ID for entry.

vocabulary.note_zh: 用于图书馆还书或归还借用品。
vocabulary.note_en: Used for returning books or borrowed items.

vocabulary.note_zh: 在韩国校园中用途广泛，如借书、打印、食堂。
vocabulary.note_en: Widely used on Korean campuses for library, printing, and cafeteria.

items.ai_explanation_en: Library interactions are practical and direct. Student ID and reservation rules are common topics.
items.ai_explanation_zh: 图书馆场景的表达实用直接，涉及学生证和预约规则。

items.situation_en: Asking about dorm rules, maintenance, visitors, and quiet hours.
items.situation_zh: 处理宿舍入住、设施问题、室友沟通和生活规则。

useful_expressions.en: I'd like to check the dorm rules.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 入住宿舍时了解管理规定。
useful_expressions.usage_en: When moving in, ask about dormitory regulations.

useful_expressions.en: Where do I submit a repair request?
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 宿舍设施坏了时询问维修方法。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Ask where to report maintenance issues in the dorm.

example_dialogue.speaker: 学生
example_dialogue.en: Where do I report a repair?

example_dialogue.speaker: 管理员
example_dialogue.zh: 请向办公室提交申请表。
example_dialogue.en: Please submit a form at the office.

vocabulary.note_zh: 韩国的大学宿舍通常设施完善，有宵禁规定。
vocabulary.note_en: Korean university dorms usually have curfews and good facilities.

vocabulary.note_zh: 指宿舍、学校或公司的规章制度。
vocabulary.note_en: Refers to regulations at dorms, schools, or companies.

vocabulary.note_zh: 用于报修或询问维修进度。
vocabulary.note_en: Used when reporting issues or asking about repair status.

items.ai_explanation_en: Dormitory Korean often involves rules and requests. Use polite request forms to sound cooperative.
items.ai_explanation_zh: 宿舍场景常涉及规则和请求，使用礼貌请求语气会让你显得更配合。

items.situation_en: Finding buildings, student offices, clubs, and campus services.
items.situation_zh: 在校园中问路、咨询办公室、参加活动或处理行政事项。

useful_expressions.en: Where is the student support center?
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 询问校园内服务机构的位置。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Ask about the location of a campus service office.

useful_expressions.en: I'd like to join a club.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 表达加入学校社团的意愿。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Express interest in joining a school club or society.

useful_expressions.en: How do I get to this building?
useful_expressions.usage_en: Ask for directions to a building on campus.

example_dialogue.speaker: 学生
example_dialogue.en: Where is the student support center?

example_dialogue.speaker: 前台
example_dialogue.zh: 在图书馆旁边的建筑二楼。
example_dialogue.en: It's on the second floor of the building next to the library.

vocabulary.note_zh: 处理国际学生事务、签证咨询、生活支援。
vocabulary.note_en: Handles international student affairs, visa consultation, and living support.

vocabulary.note_zh: 韩国大学有丰富的社团文化，学期初招新。
vocabulary.note_en: Korean universities have vibrant club cultures with recruitment at the start of each semester.

vocabulary.note_zh: 校园问路时经常用到的基础词。
vocabulary.note_en: Basic vocabulary for getting directions on campus.

items.ai_explanation_en: Campus navigation phrases are useful early in the semester. Practice location words like 옆, 앞, 뒤, and 층.
items.ai_explanation_zh: 学期初期校园导航表达非常实用，建议练习 옆（旁边）、앞（前面）、뒤（后面）、층（楼层）等词。

items.situation_en: Opening, transitioning, and closing a class presentation.
items.situation_zh: 进行课堂展示、说明观点并回应问题。

useful_expressions.usage_zh: 展示或演讲时的开场用语。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Formal opening phrase for a presentation or speech.

useful_expressions.zh: 接下来我们进入下一部分。
useful_expressions.en: Next, I'll move on to the next part.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 展示中切换话题时使用。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Transition phrase when moving between sections of a presentation.

useful_expressions.en: If you have questions, please let me know.
useful_expressions.usage_zh: 展示结束后邀请听众提问。
useful_expressions.usage_en: Invite questions at the end of a presentation.

example_dialogue.speaker: 学生
example_dialogue.en: Hello everyone. I will begin my presentation.

example_dialogue.speaker: 学生
example_dialogue.zh: 接下来我来说明研究结果。
example_dialogue.en: Next, I will explain the research results.

example_dialogue.speaker: 学生
example_dialogue.en: If you have questions, please ask.

vocabulary.note_zh: 韩国课堂中很常见，学生需定期做发表。
vocabulary.note_en: Common in Korean classrooms; students give regular presentations.

vocabulary.note_zh: 学术报告和研究发表时的关键短语。
vocabulary.note_en: Key phrase for academic reports and research presentations.

vocabulary.note_zh: 用于课堂展示时切换话题。
vocabulary.note_en: Used to transition between topics in a presentation.

items.ai_explanation_en: Presentation Korean benefits from clear signposting phrases. They help listeners follow structure.
items.ai_explanation_zh: 课堂展示中清晰的路标短语有助于听众跟上结构，是韩语课堂展示的关键技能。
